exit_criteria: Accepted decisions are explicit enough for documentation compiler.
No code changes are planned in feedback layer.
Open organization choice for gateway folders is captured as a planning/detail decision, not hidden.

loop_start.reason: Compiler loops start from source refs and build handoffs, not prior loop chat memory.
evidence: new session id or recorded context reset

validation.reason: Fresh validation is preferred; policy may require it for high-risk semantic gates.

next_loop.reason: Documentation compiler should read accepted feedback build plus KB sources, not rely on chat memory.
evidence: new session id or recorded context reset

requirements.text: Scripts are disposable developer helpers and must not contain or enforce authoritative CodeWiki product policy; source, tests, and gateways must not depend on scripts.
Bootstrap behavior is owned by the CodeWiki skill workflow or a bootstrap sub-skill; source may execute it through application tools, but skill assets own prompts/templates/workflow guidance.
Prompt templates are skill assets, not arbitrary package source assets.
The web surface is a UI under src/ui, not an agent harness adapter under src/adapters.

Domain concepts such as task, roadmap, session queue, build, validation, and state belong in src/domain; application contains compilers, gateways, state engine, and agent-facing tools that use domain concepts.
Remove public project lexicon around claim; use session queue and scoped leases under session/queue instead. Legacy codewiki_claim may remain only as a transitional adapter alias until migration.
Application should expose internal agent-callable tools in src/application/tools so Pi tools, skill helpers, CLI, or future adapters call the same use-case API.

Use application/compilers for feedback, documentation, planning, and implementation compilers; keep validation gateways separate under application/gateways by profile unless later evidence favors colocating them.
Use domain/state and application/state-engine naming; graph is generated representation, not the main domain concept.

evidence_mapping.criterion: Accepted architecture decisions compiled with requirement ids.
evidence_mapping.evidence: REQ-ARCH-001 through REQ-ARCH-009 in this feedback build.

agent_assessment: Architecture direction is coherent: domain-led model, application compiler/gateway/tool APIs, UI separated from harness adapters, and scripts demoted to optional wrappers. Main migration risk is transitional naming around existing codewiki_claim and existing src/adapters/web paths; docs should mark these as legacy aliases rather than pretending code already moved.
summary: Accepted domain-led architecture refactor: remove script authority, move bootstrap/templates/prompts to skill assets, move web UI to src/ui, make domain concepts power DDD, expose agent-facing application tools, use session queue scoped leases instead of claim lexicon.

diff_table.current_state: scripts/check-architecture.mjs currently carries product-policy checks and is invoked by tests.
diff_table.desired_state: CodeWiki authoritative checks live in source tests/gateways/audit engines; scripts are optional wrappers or can be removed.
diff_table.rationale: Disposable scripts must not become hidden product policy.

diff_table.current_state: Bootstrap/templates/prompts live in package source files without clear skill-asset ownership.
diff_table.desired_state: Skill assets define bootstrap workflow, prompts, and starter templates; source exposes execution through application tools/adapters.
diff_table.rationale: Agent workflow assets should sit with skill guidance and compile through stable tools.

diff_table.current_state: Web UI currently lives under src/adapters/web.
diff_table.desired_state: Web UI moves to src/ui/web because it is product UI, not harness adapter.
diff_table.rationale: Adapters translate harness/protocol capabilities; UI renders human product surfaces.

diff_table.current_state: Application contains many domain concepts such as session, state, roadmap, graph, claims, and builders.
diff_table.desired_state: Domain owns task/roadmap/session queue/build/validation/state concepts; application owns compilers, gateways, state-engine, and agent-facing tools.
diff_table.rationale: DDD requires domain concepts to power application workflows, not be buried inside orchestration code.

diff_table.current_state: Public lexicon and tools still use change claim terminology.
diff_table.desired_state: Project lexicon uses session queue scoped leases; legacy claim naming is transitional adapter compatibility only.
diff_table.rationale: One coordination vocabulary prevents drift between runtime state and agent guidance.

accepted_decisions.summary: Demote scripts to non-authoritative optional developer helpers or thin wrappers only.
Move bootstrap/templates/prompts ownership into CodeWiki skill assets; source calls them through application tools.
Treat web as product UI under src/ui, not an adapter.
Refactor toward domain-led DDD: domain concepts first, application compilers/gateways/state-engine/tools second.
Replace public claim vocabulary with session queue scoped lease vocabulary; keep legacy adapter alias only during migration.
Expose agent-callable internal use cases through src/application/tools.
Prefer separate application/gateways folders organized by gateway profile.
Use state naming in domain/application; graph remains generated representation.

assumptions: This build captures architecture intent only; implementation and file moves belong to planning/implementation layers.
open_questions: Exact gateway folder granularity can be finalized during planning, but separate application/gateways by profile is the current preferred default.
non_goals: Do not move source files in this feedback/documentation step.
Do not remove legacy codewiki_claim immediately without compatibility planning.
